senses.gloss: argument (for or against something); piece of evidence, proof, legal record

examples.text: وقال المتحدث باسم الكرملين ديمتري بيسكوف للصحافيين إن "السلطات الاسبانية وحلف شمال الأطلسي والصحف لم تأت بحجة واحدة ذات قيمة لدعم هذه الادعاءات".
examples.english: The speaker in the name of the Kremlin Dmitri Peskov said to the journalists that “the Spanish authorities and the North Atlantic Treaty and the papers have not come out with a single valuable piece of evidence to support these allegations [that the Russian government has interfered in the Catalonia crisis by spreading disinformation].”

senses.gloss: instance noun of حَجّ (ḥajj); hajj, pilgrimage

body.text: by which one overcomes in a litigation, dispute,
so called because recourse is had to it (
refels, an adversary in a litigation, dispute,
altercation: an argument; a plea; an allegation:
[it may be true or false: see Kur xlii. 15, and xlv. 24:] (TA:)
a proof; an evidence; a testimony:
often thus used in the present day:] also applied to a person; like
; (A and Mgh and TA in art.
He who preserves in his mind
is an evidence against him who does not;
occurring often in the larger lexicons, expressing the superior authority of hearsay, or usage, over analogy &c.; and in the saying,]
Thou art an evidence against thyself
]; a phrase mentioned by Akh: (S in art.
